acceptance_criteria_for_revision: Formulas_Export lists all critical formula strings and mappings; no hidden or named-only references remain unexplained
Record_Assessment_flat reproduces numeric results shown in memo and raw_actual_outputs (same selected_combo and cost)
Memo contains explicit example cell-coordinate tracebacks for at least three records
Deterministic precheck confirms artifacts present and accessible; verifier should be able to parse formulas export as plain text
extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: small (add export sheets, update memo, regenerate artifacts, run precheck)
extra_model_fields.notes: These changes target transparency and machine-parseability, minimizing risk to existing calculations. Keep existing worksheet formulas intact and only add explanatory/export sheets to avoid altering decision logic.

planned_changes: Add a new workbook sheet named 'Formulas_Export' that lists, in plain text, each critical formula used (Record_Assessment calculations, metric1/2/3, priority formula, sensitivity adjustments, remediation cost aggregation, and decision/expert-review flag) with the exact cell references or named-range expansions.

Add a new workbook sheet or CSV 'Record_Assessment_flat' that contains one row per embedded packet record with columns: packet_row_id, zone_mean_z, sample_n, corrected_mean, metric1, metric2, metric3, priority, action_class_base, action_class_minus5pct, action_class_plus5pct, expert_review_flag.

Update validation_note.md to include 3 explicit example tracebacks mapping Record_Assessment row(s) back to Packet_Embedded cells (e.g., 'Record_Assessment!B5 = Packet_Embedded!C3 (zone_mean_z for R2)') and note where each assumption/formula is implemented (sheet and cell range).
If any named ranges or programmatic constructs are used, expand them in Formulas_Export to absolute cell ranges to ensure parseability without named-range resolution.

Regenerate artifacts and run deterministic precheck; attach precheck output and include a short verification block in the memo confirming the exported formulas produce the same selected_combo and cost as before.
Run a quick internal sanity check (compute priority for each record in the export and confirm action class thresholds yield the same decision and that remediation combination cost checks still pass).
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Remove residual grader uncertainty by exporting exact workbook formulas and providing explicit cell-coordinate tracebacks and flattened numeric outputs so graders without spreadsheet libraries can verify logic programmatically.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables meet the rubric requirements: a formula-backed workbook, clear assumptions, sensitivity checks, and a documented final recommendation (targeted_mitigation). The primary residual risk is that the grader could not programmatically inspect workbook formulas (openpyxl unavailable), so there remains small uncertainty about exact cell-level formula strings and explicit row-to-packet cell citations.
